mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects broad economic expansion and corporate health, leading to higher earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected monthly increase in the CPI inflation rate triggers fears of aggressive monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, leading to higher discount rates and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity markets are sensitive to unexpected changes in inflation that alter Fed policy expectations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment and accelerating inflation (stagflationary pressure) signals a deteriorating economic outlook, compressing corporate profit margins and driving negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflation in the 1970s was associated with poor equity performance.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with an accuracy greater than 55% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is already restrictive, a decrease signals a policy pivot towards easing, which lowers the discount rate for future earnings and boosts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:The start of an easing cycle has historically been bullish for equities.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, months with a rate cut are not followed by statistically significant positive 20-day returns compared to months with no cut.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates directly into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: A signal constructed from real retail sales growth (nominal RSAFS growth minus CPIAUCSL growth) has a Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a short period is a reliable real-time indicator of a recession, leading to a sharp repricing of growth expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following a trigger, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A negative surprise in industrial production indicates an unexpected disruption to supply chains or production, negatively impacting near-term earnings forecasts and investor sentiment.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and tighter financial conditions, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown that negatively affects future corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive and cyclical sector.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting negative 60-day returns is below 50% over a full business cycle.
